entry.title: Re: Whitescreen after logging in Public cloud with Zebra...
entry.content:
Hallo @FlorisBoon ,

Sorry for the late reply, and thanks for your update regarding the Zebra MC3300 and Enterprise Browser.

Mobile access is supported only through SAP Fiori apps, which require a modern browser and OS in S4HANA Public cloud. Although Enterprise Browser helps with device management, it depends on the underlying Android version.

The MC3300 with Android 8.1 does not fully support SAP Fiori apps due to missing web standards like JavaScript ES6 and newer SAPUI5 components, this is why you see a white screen.

S4 Public Cloud Notes,

  • No RFUI or SAP GUI screens for mobile — only Fiori apps are supported
  • SAP recommends Android 10 or newer for full compatibility

Next Steps,

  • Check with Zebra if your device can be upgraded to Android 10+
  • Alternatively, test with a newer Zebra device validated for Fiori apps in Public Cloud
  • Let me know if you need help reviewing device options or SAP requirements.

Best regards
Chuma

entry.title: How to subscribe multi-tenant app in different global account.
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have a multitenant application hosted on SAP BTP which uses resources such as destination service and cloud connector. This works fine when our customers' subscribing subaccounts reside in the same global account that also keeps the subaccount that provides the application. I know this should be the preferred way to realize multitenant subscriptions. However, some customers would like to subscribe the app from within their own global account, i.e. a subaccount should subscribe to another subaccount in a different global account.

As described in this article service broker could be an option to do this but it comes with disadvantages (cloud connector/destinations cannot be used).

Is there another standard approach to subscribe to a multitenant app/subaccount in a different global account?

 

Many Thanks,

RK

entry.title: Re: SAC/Seamless Planning – Transactions Recorded at High...
entry.content:
@benhaddou 

The default behavior of SAC is to load data only at leaf level. Data can be manually disaggregated while planning in tables to leaf members as per  disaggregation behavior set for the dimension but data load is always on leaf member. The only way to load data to parent nodes is to first create these nodes as leaf members in alternate hierarchy and while loading the data you can turn off the validation against the hierarchy. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/1679129715024574b7bbb4a611471765.html

Point 15 in the below help section explains conditional validation against hierarchy.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/a8435da6970041d2beb3299cdcff7026.html

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2506
entry.content:
Welcome to the 2506 release of SAP Asset Performance Management (APM). 

This release brings two major innovations: native APM Transport Management powered by SAP BTP Cloud Transport Management Service (CTMS), and the ability to seamlessly consume device data ingested via the Embedded IoT platform. 

You’ll also find smarter filtering options, clearer visibility into file uploads, and new OData APIs built on SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) to support maintenance recommendations based on risk, criticality, functions, and failure modes.

And as a quick heads up we'd love you to join our new Customer Engagement Initiative Empowering Reliability: Data-Driven Maintenance Efficiency Analysis for Strategy Optimization. Collaborate with us and help us gather specific user needs and validate initial ideas for analytical tools, visualizations, and workflows that will form the foundation of a future solution! Register by July 26, 2025.

Let's take a look at release 2506.

Introducing APM Transport Management with SAP BTP Cloud Transport Management Service (CTMS)

SAP Asset Performance Management now offers its own native transport management solution using the SAP BTP Cloud Transport Management Service (CTMS), replacing previous manual processes. This centralized service lets you efficiently move configurations and templates across your connected cloud environments.

You can access the CTMS via a new app called Transport Manager.

Each system environment is represented as a node in CTMS, providing clear visibility and control over your transport landscape. Secure connections called destinations link CTMS to each tenant, while defined transport routes manage the flow of configurations to ensure data moves securely and in the correct order.

This new solution supports transporting key objects including:

  • Communication system configurations
  • Coding masks
  • Risk and criticality assessment templates
  • Strategy assessment templates for classes, including associated recommendations

To ensure smooth transports, all related SAP S/4HANA master data and dependencies should be synchronized and available in the target tenant before initiating transport.

For more information, see the SAP Help portal at:

  • Transport Management
  • Transport Communication Systems
  • Transport Coding Masks
  • Integration with SAP Cloud Transport Management service.

Watch this space for a dedicated blog and video, coming soon! 

Smarter Filtering in APM: Failure Data Profile & Risk Score Type

Customers working with technical objects in often face challenges when filtering equipment or understanding risk scores. We’ve introduced two key enhancements to improve usability and decision-making:

1. Failure Data Profile in Explore Technical Objects app

Within the Explore Technical Objects app, users can now filter more effectively using the Failure Data Profile:

  • Fuzzy search helps find matching failure patterns quickly.
  • A new column supports condition-based filtering.
  • Related technical objects are displayed instantly, making it easier to act on recurring issues.

2. Risk Score Type Filtering

Previously, risk scores were stored in mixed formats—numeric and alphanumeric—leading to sorting issues.

What’s new:
  • Users can now filter by risk score type for better clarity.
  • Sorting by ascending or descending scores works accurately.
  • Equipment without RC scores is also flagged, helping highlight missing data.

These enhancements make it easier to identify critical technical objects and understand their risk levels—supporting more effective reliability planning.

Embedded IoT - Availability of Device Connectivity Data

Ingest Data via Embedded IoT Platform provider and Consume it Seamlessly in APM

We've got great news for those working with connected assets: it's now possible to consume data in Asset Performance Management (APM) that was ingested via our Embedded IoT Platform provider.

This enhancement brings flexibility to customers using protocols like MQTT to send times series data for their assets. By leveraging embedded IoT capabilities, you can now use the data that is ingested via device protocols into the Embedded IoT provider platform in APM to support asset health monitoring, predictive maintenance, and intelligent decision-making. 

Why This Matters

Many industrial environments use lightweight messaging protocols like MQTT to collect telemetry data from machines, devices, and sensors. Embedded IoT makes it easy to ingest and process this data. Until now, this data was not yet made available for consumption in APM.

With this new capability, you can:

  • Ingest telemetry data via our embedded iot provider (e.g., using MQTT)
  • Benefit from automated data integration into APM without manual steps or interventions
  • Use APM features to monitor asset condition, define failure thresholds, and generate maintenance requests

Device connectivity data from the embedded IoT provider platform is now available in SAP Asset Performance Management for consumption as a part of the SAP Early Adopter Care Program. For more information, see Device Data Integration in SAP Asset Performance Management   To obtain access to this feature, refer to Getting Early Adopter Access to New Features and Functions.

This brief video shows how to map sensor time series to asset indicators using the Digital Twin Manager in SAP Asset Performance Management.

File Upload Status in the Integration Monitor

For customers using the Data Integration channel via File Uploads on Embedded IoT, the Integration Monitor offers a new File Upload Status page, enhancing transparency for the file upload feature introduced last year.

Together, these features provide tools to customize, visualize, secure, connect, and optimize your assets, turning complex data into clear insights and confident actions—so your assets perform at their best. 

New OData APIs as part of the CAP transition

As part of the transition to the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), we’ve introduced new OData APIs to support more flexible and future-ready integrations. These APIs are designed to help determine maintenance recommendations based on risk, criticality, functions, and failure modes. See https://api.sap.com/api/recommendationOdata/overview

 

Stay Informed and Get Involved

  • For an exhaustive list of all new features, visit the SAP Help Portal - What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management.. 
  • Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session.
  • Collaborate with us! Don't forget to register by July 26, 2025 for our new Customer Engagement Initiative Empowering Reliability: Data-Driven Maintenance Efficiency Analysis for Strategy Optimization. We'd love to have you on board! 

See you in the next release!

entry.title: DTW logs sometimes fails on italian server
entry.content:
Dear Sirs,
we are experiencing an issue with the Data Transfer Workbench (DTW) logs.

When we import documents using DTW, everything appears to work correctly during the session, the DTW interface displays the imported rows and  the errors if any .
However, once we close the DTW application, no entries are saved into the dtw.mdb log file.

We have performed several tests, and the problem appears to be related to the date format or system date. Our system is configured with Italian regional settings (dd/MM/yyyy).

Steps to reproduce:
-Set the PC date to 01 July 2025.
-Set regional settings to Italy (or just configure the date format to dd/MM/yyyy).
-Import any object using DTW (e.g., an Items or a Business Partner).
-Close the DTW interface.
-Open the file located at: %localappdata%\SAP\SAP Business One\Log\Data Transfer Workbench\dtw.mdb

You will notice that:
No records are saved in OLOG or LOG1 tables for the last import.

Note:
If we change the system date to June 30th, 2025 and repeat the same procedure, the log is created correctly.

Could you please confirm if this is a known issue or limitation?
If there is a workaround or a fix available?
Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Marco

entry.title: Smart Resolve in Action for Business Support
entry.content:
The ability to be smart refers to responding quickly and intelligently in challenging situations. Smart systems do justice to that as they gather historical information that leads to intelligent inferences and subsequent actions or solutions. When it comes to Smart Resolve capabilities, the vast array of possibilities can be illustrated by airplanes, for example. The wings adapt to the environmental information they receive, such as altitude, speed, and air temperature, to maintain a safe flight for all to reach the destination.

This concept is not limited to aviation, however, as it encompasses other industries, where these capabilities play a role in everyday operations. This need for intelligence for businesses to run smoothly is highly prominent during business-critical periods, when continuity demands operational efficiency. Smart Resolve capabilities are built on this principle. Developed to improve support processes through intelligence and automation, they involve close front office work with support teams to ensure customer readiness during sales events.

The main goal of Smart Resolve is to reduce engineer effort for case resolution and case volume through the deflection and prevention of issues before they impact businesses. As automation is introduced at various stages of case handling, the technical innovations that guide the Smart Resolve initiative enable the Product Support organization to resolve customer issues more efficiently.

As part of SAP’s efforts towards proactive support, Smart Resolve does holistic 360-degree checks. Validated by engineers for accuracy, it is deployed to troubleshoot specific failures within customer environments.

The Technology Behind Smart Resolve

Smart Resolve applies SAP technologies, including:

  • SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA):
    This low-code/no-code platform allows support engineers to design automations that replicate complex business logic and human actions (such as mouse clicks, data entry, and more). Operating within SAP’s secure Virtual Desktop Instances, SBPA promotes flexibility and compatibility across various technology stacks. Its integration with SAP Business Technology Platform opens the door to advanced AI functionalities.
    • SAP BTP AI Core:
    Through SAP BTP GenAI Hub, Smart Resolve seizes up to date AI models and capabilities. One highlight is the use of Retrieval-Augmented Generation architecture, which offers AI-driven analysis and recommendations based on SAP’s knowledge base which include relevant technical and functional documentations and knowledge base articles. This fusion of automation and AI allows extensive insights and rapid, data-driven decision-making.

Smart Resolve in Action within Technical Support

Several automation use cases bring the following benefits within SAP Product Support:

  • Auto Sending Adobe Credentials to Customers:
    In place of customers creating a support case to request new Adobe Reader Rights credentials, an automation has been implemented to address this challenge. A bot runs on an hourly schedule to identify open cases related to credential requests. For each case, the bot generates the credentials and sends them to customers, which reduces manual effort and turnaround time.
  • Follow-up Note on Cases in "Awaiting Info":
    To avoid auto-closure of customer cases that haven’t seen an update in 21 days, a bot runs once daily. It scans the ServiceNow case list and posts an external update to any qualifying cases inactive for 14 days. This proactive follow-up speeds customer responses before the 21-day auto-closure window, improving case resolution rates and saving approximately 37 hours of engineer effort.
  • Cloud ALM – Automatic Case Responder:
    The Cloud ALM team receives about 200 Solution Manager test cases monthly, all requiring manual responses. A bot now monitors incoming cases, attaches a standardized template response, and returns the case to the customer automatically. This has enhanced time-to-solve, reduced backlog, and saved a total of 4050 hours in processing delays.
  • Development Support’s Engineers Notification:
    An hourly automation identifies customer-closed cases assigned to Development Support and notifies the Product Support engineer who forwarded them. Through this procedure, the valuable insights and solutions, that often emerge during cases forwarded to DS teams, will be captured in any customer-facing resource by the time the case is closed. Besides, it decreases ping-pong case, while solution accuracy is strengthened.

Innovation for 2025: Proactive and Preventative Support

For 2025, we are introducing Smart Resolve Proactive Case Creation. A case is created by Product Support as soon as an issue is identified in customer environments. Through automated executions and periodic reviews, Subject Matter experts determine the next step required and the support solution to prevent any substantial business impact. Proactive Case Creation is triggered when the automation encounters a failure or a misconfiguration in the tenant. The checks from the automation supplementation, with AI based recommendations, help the engineers to more confidently address the issue. While the customer is notified through the case, the situation is decisively de-escalated, and hypercare support is offered.

Moreover, Preventative Customer Risk Mitigation (PCRM) is another solution designed to avoid disruption. Based on automation and agentic AI capabilities, it recognizes and proactively reduces risks, as Customer Support experts redefine the issue-solving approach from a reactive to a more proactive one. Additionally, 24x7 control room support with live monitoring, risk mitigation, and on-site staffing across global hubs set the stage for quick adoption across multiple products.
As automation is implemented at multiple phases of issue identification and analysis, AI agents assemble information collected from multiple sources, then summarize it into an LLM based response reviewed by SAP experts. Once approved, a well-designed agent workflow is triggered to address specific business scenarios

Real-World Impact: Integration and Availability

These innovations, within the scope of Smart Resolve, consolidate systems along with custom automations that monitor applications, collect logs and configurations, and perform holistic 360° analyses. During Black Friday and Cyber Monday, for example, these tools run preventatively in the weeks leading up to the event, allowing SAP teams to create actionable plans for problems that might jeopardize e-commerce activities before they arise.

For customers participating in the Smart Resolve initiative, the hundreds of hours of preventative support given before sales events were decisive in maintaining system functionality, reducing engineer effort, and increasing detection of potentially impactful issues. Like pilots who guide aircraft safely to their destinations, SAP teams work relentlessly with Smart Resolve capabilities, not just to deliver secure and reliable customer journeys, but also to achieve success for our customers’ core businesses.

A sincere appreciation to Nivedhaa Murali, Senior Solution Engineer, and Jashan Dhaliwal, Smart Resolve Business Lead, SAP experts who have contributed to this article.

entry.title: Re: Miss match in catalogs and apps between starter and D...
entry.content:
Hello @Inchara_BK 

Thank you for your follow-up question. For the S/4HANA Public Cloud (3SL setup), here’s how to differentiate between Demo apps and Productive apps.

  • The Starter System includes demo apps, additional catalogues, and workshop content not intended for productive use
  • The Development, Test, and Production systems only show apps linked to your activated scope items and assigned business roles

How to Check,

  • Demo apps often appear only in the Starter System and belong to catalogues not tied to active scope items
  • In your Development system, after regenerating roles from templates, only productive apps relevant to your scope should remain
  • You can use the Fiori App Library to search by App ID or Business Catalogue to verify if the app belongs to your active scope

SAP References,

  1. SAP Best Practice Scope Items – Process Navigator
  2. SAP Fiori Apps Library – S/4HANA Public Cloud
  3. SAP Help – Role and Catalogue Setup for S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: namespace for /CFCRMX2/ GTS system
entry.content:
To obtain namespace for /CFCRMX2/ GTS system, please follow the steps below:

  1. Go to SAP for Me > Systems & Provisioning > Keys > Namespaces card
  2. Click Create Namespaces
  3. Enter a Namespace name with 3-8 characters. Note: If the name entered, has been used by another customer, it will not be possible to continue.
  4. Enter Description
  5. Click Create
  6. Read through the 'Agreement Relating to Namespace Reservation' screen that is presented, and click I agree and click Confirm
  7. Click Create and it is created immediately
  8. The Namespace overview page will be presented. There are several options here:
    • The Customer Data is visible at the top
    • Click Edit top right to change the Responsible S-user and/or Namespace description
    • Click Add External Assignment to reassign the namespace to another customer and installation number outside your company (see KBA 1997646)
    • Click Reassign Owner to move the namespace to another customer and installation number outside of your company (see KBA 3004591)
  9. Under CREATE KEY, click the 'key icon' for the desired installation number to create Producer/Development and Customer/Repair key
  10. The Developer and Repair Keys are generated automatically

For a more detailed information, please refer to KBA 2006427 - How to request a Development Namespace - SAP for Me

If you encounter any issues or need further assistance, please do not hesitate to reach out.

SAP Support
Judy

@Prasenjitsbist 

When you have logged on to the Cloud Foundry (as the error message states), a destination pointing to the ABAP trial environment will be automatically created at the initial access time. Then you can select this destination, after which OData services registered there will be loaded. 

From my experience, loading OData services from the ABAP trial environment takes a lot of time (~20 minutes).

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Calibration in SAP SuccessFactors: A Strategic Guide for HR Leaders
entry.content:
With appraisal season around the corner, many organizations are facing the same recurring questions: How do we ensure everyone is evaluated fairly? How do we make these sessions more meaningful? How do we move beyond just filling out forms to discussing talent and development?

As I'm currently working closely with several organizations through their calibration cycles, I thought this would be a good time to share some practical insights and key strategies that have proven effective. These experiences have highlighted a few important differentiators between organizations that maximize their calibration investment and those that treat it as a routine administrative task.

This guide outlines proven strategies for maximizing calibration ROI, demonstrates advanced integration with succession and compensation modules, and reveals how calibration data can drive organizational agility through strategic talent insights.

The Foundation: Best Practices for Running Effective Calibration Sessions

1. Pre-Calibration Preparation: The 48-Hour Rule

The Challenge: Many calibration sessions fail because managers arrive unprepared, leading to surface-level discussions and biased decisions.

The Solution: Implement a structured 48-hour preparation protocol:

  • Data Validation: Ensure all performance forms are complete with supporting documentation
  • Talent Profile Review: Verify that employee profiles include current competencies, development goals, and career aspirations
  • Manager Briefing: Share calibration guidelines, rating distribution targets, and session objectives
  • System Check: Confirm all participants have appropriate permissions and can access calibration views

Pro Tip: Create a calibration readiness checklist within SAP SF using the Forms module to track preparation completion across all participants.

2. Strategic View Selection: Beyond the Default Dashboard

Many organizations default to the Dashboard View, missing opportunities for deeper talent insights. Here's when to use each view:

Dashboard View: Perfect for executive-level sessions focused on overall rating distribution and compliance

  • Best for: Senior leadership reviews, HR analytics, and organizational trending

Bin View: Ideal for identifying rating outliers and ensuring consistency

  • Best for: Manager-level sessions, performance coaching discussions, and rating normalization

Matrix Grid: The powerhouse for talent discussions combining multiple data points

  • Best for: Succession planning sessions, high-potential identification, and development planning

3. Facilitate Meaningful Dialogue: The STAR Method for Calibration

Transform calibration conversations using the STAR method:

  • Situation: What was the employee's context and challenges?
  • Task: What specific goals or projects were they responsible for?
  • Action: How did they approach their responsibilities?
  • Result: What was the measurable impact of their performance?

System Leverage: Use SAP SF's comment tracking feature to document STAR discussions, creating a rich talent history that supports future development and succession decisions.

Beyond Performance: Strategic Integration with Succession and Compensation

Succession Planning Integration: Building Tomorrow's Leaders Today

Calibration's impact extends far beyond performance ratings when integrated with succession planning data. By combining calibrated performance ratings with succession risk assessments, organizations can create a holistic talent review process that identifies:

  • Ready-now successors with proven performance track records
  • Leadership gaps where succession planning needs immediate attention
  • High-potential talent requiring accelerated development pathways

The key is to use calibration outcomes to inform succession planning decisions, ensuring that leadership pipelines are built on objective performance data rather than subjective assessments alone.

Compensation Alignment: Where Performance Meets Pay

Calibrated ratings become powerful when they inform fair and competitive compensation decisions. The integration allows organizations to:

  • Balance performance with potential when making pay decisions
  • Ensure equity across different departments and roles
  • Align compensation strategies with calibrated performance outcomes

This integrated approach helps break down silos between HR functions, driving unified workforce planning that considers both current performance and future potential.

Talent Flags: Enriching Calibration Discussions

Incorporating talent flags such as "high potential," "flight risk," or "ready for stretch assignments" enriches calibration discussions by providing additional context for decision-making. These flags enable:

  • Targeted development strategies for high-potential employees
  • Retention interventions for flight-risk talent
  • Succession planning accuracy through better talent identification

Data-Driven Decision Making: From Calibration to Business Impact

Workforce Analytics: Turning Calibration Data into Strategic Insights

Calibrated ratings become powerful when aggregated for organizational intelligence:

Key Metrics to Track:

  • Performance distribution by department, role, and tenure
  • Succession readiness across critical positions
  • High-potential talent pipeline strength
  • Development needs concentration areas

Advanced Analytics: Use SAP SF's People Analytics capabilities to create predictive models based on calibrated data, forecasting talent needs and identifying potential performance risks.

Talent Development: Personalized Pathways Powered by Calibration

The Strategic Approach: Use calibration outcomes to create targeted development interventions:

  • Top Performers: Stretch assignments and leadership development programs
  • Solid Contributors: Skill enhancement and cross-functional exposure
  • Improvement Needed: Intensive coaching and performance improvement plans

Advanced Configuration Tips for Maximum Impact

1. Optimize Calibration Session Settings

Configure your calibration sessions with appropriate user permissions and access controls. Ensure that managers have the right level of access to view and modify ratings while maintaining data security.

2. Leverage Calibration Reports

Use SAP SuccessFactors' built-in calibration reports to track rating distributions, identify outliers, and monitor session progress. These reports provide valuable insights into calibration effectiveness.

3. Configure Appropriate Calibration Views

Set up different calibration views based on your organizational needs—whether focusing on performance ratings, potential assessments, or combined talent matrix discussions.

4. Enable Collaborative Features

Make use of the commenting and discussion features within calibration sessions to document decisions and rationale, creating a valuable record for future reference and development planning.

Key Takeaways for Immediate Action

Implement the 48-hour preparation protocol to ensure meaningful calibration sessions

Choose calibration views strategically based on session objectives and audience

Integrate succession and compensation data for holistic talent discussions

Leverage talent flags for targeted development and retention strategies

Create analytics dashboards to track calibration impact on business outcomes

Configure automated workflows to ensure consistent process execution

Your Next Steps

Calibration is more than a compliance exercise—it's your gateway to strategic talent management. By following these best practices and leveraging advanced integration capabilities, you can transform your SAP SuccessFactors calibration process into a competitive advantage.

What's your biggest calibration challenge? I'd love to hear about your experiences and discuss strategies for overcoming common implementation hurdles. Connect with me to continue the conversation and explore how these approaches can work in your unique organizational context.

Have you implemented innovative calibration strategies in your organization? Share your success stories and lessons learned in the comments below—let's build a community of practice around strategic talent management.

SAPSuccessFactors Calibration TalentManagement SuccessionPlanning PerformanceManagement HRTech WorkforceAnalytics LeadershipDevelopment PeopleStrategy DigitalHR

entry.title: Set Custom Response Headers in Cloud Foundry Environment
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

For context, we have an ABAP Environment Service running on the Cloud Foundry environment. Within this Service, we have deployed multiple of our own custom applications. These applications were built and are being developed using SAP BAS.

We'd like to set some custom response headers to any request coming to our service.

So far, we have tried setting them on one of our custom apps using

<meta http-equiv [...]>

and also by adding a xs-app.json file in the same folder as our manifest.json file, adding only the following option, as detailed here:

{ "responseHeaders" : [
    {"name": "header1", "value": "value1"},
    {"name": "header2", "value": "value2"}
  ]
}

But this didn't have the desired effect app-wide, let alone instance-wide.

Our question is how exactly do we set custom response headers? Preferably instance-wide, but at the very least app-wide. Is the only solution using an unmanaged application router? And if so, what are the steps we should take bearing in mind that up until now we defined our routing in each custom app's manifest.json file?

entry.title: Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile: Getting Things Done with AI on the
entry.content:
With the 1H 2025 SAP SuccessFactors release, our AI copilot Joule is now available in the SAP SuccessFactors mobile app - offering a more accessible, efficient way for employees and managers to complete everyday HR tasks, no matter where they are.

A More Natural Mobile Experience

Whether you’re a manager reviewing team updates or an employee checking on a request, Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile is designed to meet you in the flow of work.

 

  • Managers can:
    • Review and approve time-off or kick off bonus payments
    • Access compensation insights about the team
    • Give feedback and check goal progress with just a few taps

 

  • Employees can:
    • Find answers to HR questions (e.g. “What’s this on my pay slip?”)
    • Track learning progress and view benefits without navigating deep menus
    • Use Siri voice commands to open the app and launch Joule hands-free

It’s a simpler, more intuitive way to get work done - anytime, from anywhere.

 

 

 

Smarter, Faster Support

Joule brings the same intelligent support experience from desktop to mobile. Using advanced AI and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), it pulls answers from trusted sources like company policies, help documents, and HR systems.

Whether you're an employee needing clarity or a manager making decisions, Joule enables:

  • Up to 90% faster task execution
  • Up to 95% faster access to information

This reduces delays and allows everyone to stay focused on what matters.

Designed for Flexibility and Self-Sufficiency

From guiding users through tasks to offering step-by-step instructions, Joule helps reduce confusion and encourages confidence in handling HR processes independently.

  • Managers save time by skipping manual steps and gaining quick access to the right information
  • Employees feel more in control with answers and actions readily available - without waiting on support

With Siri voice integration, users can launch Joule without unlocking their phone, adding another layer of speed and accessibility.

 

Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile is now live with the 1H 2025 release. Simply update your app to start exploring how AI can help you work smarter - whether you’re managing a team or managing your day.

Where to find more information:

  • Check out all available use cases 
  • Setting Up Joule in SAP SuccessFactors
  • Joule Monthly Release Update
  • View documentation to help you get started
  • Getting started with SAP SuccessFactors
  • SAP SuccessFactors community
